moves.name: Stand LP
moves.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; high active frame count makes it a useful meaty (up to +8/+1); SA2 cancel: +8/+1; DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

moves.name: Stand LP
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

moves.name: Stand MP
moves.notes: Strong combo/pressure starter and meaty tool, especially out of Drive Rush to minimize pushback; meaty timing can be up to +10/+5; SA2 cancel: +14/+9; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

moves.name: Stand MP
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; 2f shorter hitconfirm window when chained into; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

moves.name: Stand HP
moves.notes: Forces stand on hit; good whiff punish tool with long range; large hurtbox makes it weak as an anti-air; SA2 cancel: +18/+14

moves.name: Stand HP
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); forces stand on hit; maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; 1f shorter hitconfirm window when chained into

moves.name: Stand LK
moves.notes: Good range for a light normal, but can't combo into 214MK; SA2 cancel: +5/0; special cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

moves.name: Stand LK
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; special cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

moves.name: Stand MK
moves.notes: 8f startup for 2nd hit only; 1st hit can combo from 5LP and has enough hitstun to combo to 214HK for a launch and Fuha stock; great poke in neutral (1st hit can interrupt opponent's button while 2nd hit extends for long range); very effective when used out of Drive Rush; SA2 cancel: +19/+7 (1st hit)

moves.name: Stand MK
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); 1st hit has 4f less hitstun (cannot combo into 214HK); when chained into, 4f shorter hitconfirm window (2f if 2nd hit only); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents

moves.name: Stand HK
moves.notes: Not airborne despite the animation; Counter-Hit/Punish Counter puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; Punish Counter also puts grounded opponents into limited juggle state with a follow-up juggle; long range and safe on block makes it great in neutral; better frame advantage when well spaced (very difficult to connect with the final active frame); extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

moves.name: Stand HK
moves.notes: Becomes fully cancelable during FSE, even when not chained into; not airborne despite the animation; maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; 2f shorter hitconfirm window when chained into; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 5th recovery frame; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

moves.name: Crouch LP
moves.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; SA2 cancel: +7/+2; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

moves.name: Crouch LP
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

moves.name: Crouch MP
moves.notes: 1f extra recovery on whiff and extends a wider hurtbox until the end of recovery; good combo tool and useful when buffered in neutral; good anti-air for horizontal approaches (hitbox is above her extended fist); SA2 cancel: +15/+8

moves.name: Crouch MP
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); 1f extra recovery on whiff and extends a wider hurtbox until the end of recovery; maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; 2f shorter hitconfirm window when chained into

moves.name: Crouch HP
moves.notes: Forces stand on hit; great anti-air but cannot hit cross-up (upward leg hurtbox is anti-air invincible on frames 8-15); Punish Counter puts airborne opponents into spinning free juggle state; all active frames are cancelable; powerful combo starter after a canceled Drive Rush hitconfirm; SA2 cancel: +22/+8

moves.name: Crouch HP
moves.notes: Chained version of 2HP during SA2 (FSE); jump cancelable on hit only; when jump canceled, FSE air normals cause juggle state; 2f shorter CHAIN react window when chained into

moves.name: Crouch LK
moves.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; becomes fully cancelable during Feng Shui Engine

moves.name: Crouch LK
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); 2f shorter special hitconfirm window when chained into; maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents

moves.name: Crouch MK
moves.notes: Excellent range for a cancelable low poke in neutral; SA2 cancel: +15/+8; Range: 1.517 (1st frame: 1.446); DR cancel is delayed until after active frames; has an extended hurtbox on whiff until the end of recovery

moves.name: Crouch MK
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); 1f shorter hitconfirm window when chained into; maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; Range: 1.517 (1st frame: 1.446); DR cancel is delayed until after active frames; has an extended hurtbox on whiff until the end of recovery

moves.name: Slash Kick
moves.notes: Becomes fully cancelable during Feng Shui Engine (even when not chained into)

moves.name: Slash Kick
moves.notes: Chained version of 2HP during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; 4f shorter hitconfirm window when chained into

moves.name: Jump LP
moves.notes: During FSE forward jump, can cancel into divekick

moves.name: Jump LP
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents

moves.name: Jump MP
moves.notes: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; cancelable into divekick; instant j.MP > j.214KK is a useful option against corner throw loops (much less punishable than a blocked reversal); can only be used as a fuzzy instant overhead if the opponent is in Burnout and blocks a deep j.HK

moves.name: Jump MP
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents

moves.name: Jump HP
moves.notes: Cross-up; causes a spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents with no Hard Knockdown; better KD Adv. when hitting lower to the ground; Juri's collision box prevents her from connecting this on grounded opponents late in her jump arc (cannot use in safe jump setups); during FSE forward jump, can cancel into divekick; lower body projectile invuln 12-23f (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Jump HP
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); lower body projectile invuln 12-23f

moves.name: Jump LK
moves.notes: Cross-up; can be used as a fuzzy instant overhead (vs. Blanka, will not work after perfect safe jump j.HK timing); during FSE forward jump, can cancel into divekick

moves.name: Jump LK
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents

moves.name: Jump MK
moves.notes: Cross-up; during FSE forward jump, can cancel into divekick; lower body projectile invuln 6-20f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Jump MK
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 6-20f except around attack hitbox

moves.name: Jump HK
moves.notes: During FSE forward jump, can cancel into divekick; lower body projectile invuln 10-13f and kicking leg 10-19f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Jump HK
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 10-13f and kicking leg 10-19f

moves.name: Neutral Jump HK
moves.notes: Hits on both sides (can cross-up if opponent tries to move underneath Juri); can be used as an air-to-air; when chained after FSE nj.MP, puts airborne opponents into juggle state; lower body projectile invuln 10-13f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Neutral Jump HK
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 10-13f

moves.name: Kyosesho
moves.notes: SA2 cancel: +16/+9

moves.name: Kyosesho
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); 2f shorter hitconfirm window when chained into; maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents

moves.name: Senkai Kick
moves.notes: Not airborne despite the animation; 2 extra recovery frames on whiff

moves.name: Senkai Kick
moves.notes: Chained Feng Shui Engine version; not airborne despite the animation; the special/chain cancel window only applies when chaining into 6MK and the KD doesn't allow anything to juggle on hit; 2 extra recovery frames on whiff; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

moves.name: Renko Kicks
moves.notes: Both hits are cancelable; 23f startup for 2nd hit only; 1st hit has more hitstun and blockstun for cancels; both hits put airborne opponent into limited juggle state; carries counter-hit/Punish Counter advantage through both hits; SA2 cancel: +18/+8 (1st hit), +17/+11 (2nd hit)

moves.name: Renko Kicks
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); maintains juggle state vs. airborne opponents; 4f shorter hitconfirm window when chained into; canceling the 1st hit uses less juggle potential, allowing alternate juggle routes in exchange for some initial damage

moves.name: Korenzan
moves.notes: Both hits are cancelable; 1st hit forces stand and puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; 2nd hit causes OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents; 19f startup for 2nd hit only; carries counter-hit/Punish Counter advantage through both hits; SA2 cancel: +12/+6 (1st hit), +16/+8 (2nd hit)

moves.name: Korenzan
moves.notes: Chained version during SA2 (FSE); both hits knock down; 2nd hit causes OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents; 8f shorter hitconfirm window when chained into

moves.name: Death Crest 1
moves.notes: Safe Target Combo starter that leaves ample time to confirm the final hit; leaves a 2f blockstring gap after 5MP

moves.name: Death Crest 2
moves.notes: (refers to scaled damage); not airborne despite the animation; always a true blockstring from 4HP; puts opponent into limited juggle state; cannot cancel into Super on block

moves.name: Spider Fang Throw
moves.notes: Has access to corner throw loops by dashing (+3); a successful Throw Escape builds 1 Drive bar and 1000 Super gauge (10% bar); Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

moves.name: Shadow Kick Throw
moves.notes: No throw oki after back throwing opponent into corner, but can use meaty strikes; a successful Throw Escape builds 1 Drive bar and 1000 Super gauge (10% bar); Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

moves.name: Zanka-sen
moves.notes: KD Adv. varies based on height (lower height = worse advantage); switches sides; no oki after throwing opponent back into the corner

moves.name: Sekkan Kick
moves.notes: See Drive Impact. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

moves.name: Kaita Raid (Block)
moves.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze; see Drive Reversal.

moves.name: Drive Parry
moves.notes: See Drive Parry. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

moves.name: Parry Drive Rush
moves.notes: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.641 (min/throw), 2.479 (min/block), 3.999 (max/final DR frame)

moves.name: Drive Rush Cancel
moves.notes: See Drive Rush; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

moves.name: Fuhajin
moves.notes: Gains 1 Fuha stock on hit/block/whiff on frame 10; 1-hit projectile clash hitbox on frames 8-13; puts opponent into limited juggle state (only combos into SA3); does not move forward during startup; 2f extra recovery on whiff; 8f hitstop when clashing with projectile

moves.name: Fuhajin
moves.notes: Gains 1 Fuha stock on hit/block/whiff on frame 13; 1-hit projectile clash hitbox on frames 11-16; puts opponent into limited juggle state (only combos into SA3); takes small step forward, making it more consistent in combos (but does not combo naturally from 5LK); 8f hitstop when clashing with projectile

moves.name: Fuhajin
moves.notes: Gains 1 Fuha stock on hit/block/whiff on frame 25; 1-hit projectile clash hitbox on frames 23-28; launches opponent high into a limited juggle state; takes large step forward (but only combos naturally from 5HP, 2HP, and first hit of 5MK and 6HP); 2f extra recovery on block; 8f hitstop when clashing with projectile

moves.name: Fuhajin
moves.notes: Gains 1 Fuha stock on hit/block/whiff on frame 12; 2-hit OD projectile clash hitbox on frames 10-17; launches opponent high into a limited juggle state; takes small step forward (consistent like the MK version while comboing naturally from light normals); SA2 cancel: KD+49/-8; 8f hitstop when clashing with projectile

moves.name: Saihasho
moves.notes: 1-hit projectile hitbox (can lose to projectile-invuln moves); very short range

moves.name: Saihasho
moves.notes: 1-hit projectile; costs 1 Fuha stock; can press MK or HK to cancel into other 236K specials for 1 Fuha stock (within the first 3 active frames)

moves.name: Saihasho
moves.notes: 2-hit OD projectile; only the 2nd hit knocks down; can press MK or HK to cancel into other OD 236K specials for 1 Fuha stock and 1 Drive bar; SA2 cancel: KD+57/+17

moves.name: Ankensatsu
moves.notes: 8-27f low crush; KD vs. airborne opponents

moves.name: Ankensatsu
moves.notes: Costs 1 Fuha stock; 8-27f low crush; KD vs. airborne opponents; can press LK or HK to cancel into other 236K specials for 1 Fuha stock

moves.name: Ankensatsu
moves.notes: 8-27f low crush; OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents (limited juggle state); pressing LK or HK cancels to other OD 236K specials for 1 Fuha stock + 1 Drive bar; SA2 cancel: +16/-3

moves.name: Go Ohsatsu
moves.notes: Costs 1 Fuha stock; both hits are Super cancelable; can press LK or MK to cancel into other 236K specials for 1 Fuha stock; puts opponent into limited juggle state (only airborne hit launches high enough for follow-up juggle besides SA3 cancel); good for damage when comboing into SA3

moves.name: Go Ohsatsu
moves.notes: All 3 hits are Super cancelable; first hit keeps opponent grounded, 2nd and 3rd hits put opponent into limited juggle state; can press LK or MK to cancel any hit into other OD 236K specials for 1 Fuha stock and 1 Drive bar; SA2 cancel: +17/+17 (1st hit), KD+54/+15 (2nd hit), KD+52/+4 (3rd hit); 3rd hit cannot juggle into held SA2 dash attack

moves.name: Tensenrin
moves.notes: Both hits are SA3 cancelable; not airborne; fairly consistent combo ender

moves.name: Tensenrin
moves.notes: Strong anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; slightly less horizontal range than 623HP

moves.name: Tensenrin
moves.notes: Only 1 hit connects vs. crouch block (4f worse oB, less chip damage); strong combo ender and anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; slightly more horizontal range than 623MP

moves.name: Tensenrin
moves.notes: Juri's primary reversal option; final attack comes out on hit only; cannot hit cross-up

moves.name: Shiku-sen
moves.notes: During forward jump only; can input follow-up on hit only; puts opponent into limited juggle state (lower connect allows better follow-up juggles); button strength does not affect trajectory

moves.name: Shiren-sen
moves.notes: Comes out on hit only; can delay the input to get better KD Advantage; no follow-up after juggle state; Juri is in a crouching state during landing recovery; total recovery time before landing varies by juggle height

moves.name: OD Shiku-sen
moves.notes: During forward jump only; can input follow-up on hit only; puts opponent into limited juggle state

moves.name: OD Shiren-sen
moves.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; comes out on hit only; can delay the input to get better juggle follow-ups and better KD Adv.; Juri is in a crouching state during landing recovery; total recovery time before landing varies by juggle height

moves.name: Sakkai Fuhazan
moves.notes: Projectile hitbox; active frames and KD Adv. are highly variable based on screen position; Full Dmg distribution: 200x4,300,700

moves.name: Sakkai Fuhazan (hold)
moves.notes: Projectile hitbox; requires one Fuha stock to use (consumes this stock); active frames and KD Adv. are highly variable based on screen position; Full Dmg distribution: 200x4,300,1000

moves.name: Feng Shui Engine
moves.notes: Lasts 600 frames (10 seconds); timer stops counting down during attack hit/block freeze; allows Juri to chain into normals of equal or ascending strength (cannot chain back into the same attack); chains change the properties of certain moves (6MK and Heavy normals become knockdowns); 5HK/2LK/2HK/6MK become cancelable

moves.name: Feng Shui Engine (hold)
moves.notes: Holding the button performs a dash attack; puts airborne opponents into free juggle state or maintains existing juggle state; can cancel on hit/block into any normal; afterwards, has same properties as regular Feng Shui Engine activation; dash attack cannot hit cross-up

moves.name: Kaisen Dankai Raku
moves.notes: Available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.4 Drive bars for Juri
